commands.review_summary:
The PR removes Google plugin mappings that force `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` to `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` and updates one Google model-id helper test.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +1. Total +1 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main still has `normalizeGooglePreviewModelId` mapping `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` to the preview id, and model-selection tests assert that behavior for Google and Vertex. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor action for real behavior proof and a focused code update across remaining model-normalization surfaces before normal review can continue.

target.title: fix(google): stop forcing preview suffix for gemini-3.1-flash-lite GA release
target.body:
## Summary

Stops forcing the `-preview` suffix for `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` since Google GA'd the model in May 2026.

## Problem

OpenClaw hardcodes `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` → `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`, redirecting users who configure the GA model to the older preview endpoint.

## Fix

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `extensions/google/model-id.ts` | Remove forced preview mapping; keep explicit `-preview` alias passthrough |
| `extensions/google/model-id.test.ts` | Update test to expect GA name unchanged |
| `extensions/google/openclaw.plugin.json` | Remove alias mapping from all 3 providers (google, google-gemini-cli, google-vertex) |

Users who explicitly want the older preview endpoint can still use `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`.

Fixes: #86151
target.author: linux2010
